response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js and D-language experience, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements: his professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 (plus a thesis project), far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He also lacks SQL Server/Oracle experience (MySQL/MongoDB instead) and has no fixed-income or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was retail investment fintech, not investment banking. The mismatch on all mandatory criteria makes him a poor fit despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: 15+ years of broad professional software development experience across web, backend, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and active open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014–2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed-income, derivatives, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and seniority
